name: Oversized base image
summary: Heavy base images increase transfer, storage, and patching footprint without improving runtime value.
description: Using a large general-purpose base image when a smaller runtime image would suffice adds waste to build, deployment, and vulnerability management workflows.

impact.cost: Larger images cost more to store and distribute.
impact.performance: Longer image pull times delay startup.
impact.carbon: Unnecessary transfer and storage increase resource use.

remediation.guidance: Prefer minimal runtime images that still meet operational needs.
examples: Use slim or distroless images when practical and compatible with debugging and support needs.
